takeaways.title: One episode can become a real benchmark contract
takeaways.readout: The public sample is converted into 5,821 frames, 1,161 aligned 20-frame windows, and an 8,546-dimensional feature contract.
takeaways.current_scope: This benchmark defines the task contract; cross-episode generalization is evaluated in the multi-episode stage.

takeaways.title: Chronological splits expose action-class shift
takeaways.readout: Earlier all-feature action classifiers reach high macro-F1 on their local split, but the 12-task chronological action/subtask heads are much harder because later held-out windows include unseen labels.
takeaways.current_scope: This split is useful for studying label shift; broad action-recognition conclusions need held-out episodes.

takeaways.title: Small neural heads help dynamic and temporal probes
takeaways.readout: The MLP heads substantially improve hand trajectory forecasting, temporal-order verification, and motion/visual synchronization.
takeaways.current_scope: These gains are measured within one episode and are candidates for held-out-episode testing.

takeaways.title: Retrieval and reconstruction remain the harder multimodal problems
takeaways.readout: Ridge/cosine retrieval remains stronger than the neural projection on this sample, and cross-modal reconstruction still has negative R2.
takeaways.current_scope: The current reconstruction task predicts feature vectors; depth, mesh, NeRF, and Gaussian-splatting outputs are future task variants.

takeaways.title: Audio helps some tasks and hurts others on the public sample
takeaways.readout: Audio improves the primary metric on 6 of 12 tasks, while raw log-mel replacement improves over the current handcrafted block on 6 of 12 tasks. The largest current-audio gain appears in feature reconstruction, not in action classification.
takeaways.current_scope: This is a single-episode ablation over fixed ridge heads. It validates that audio is wired into the task suite and shows where it changes metrics; it does not prove cross-episode audio generalization.

takeaways.title: The next scientific unit is held-out episodes, not more adjacent windows
takeaways.readout: The selected Qwen3-Omni path now has a verified two-epoch held-out diagnostic result. It proves the cross-episode train/validation/eval loop and meets the strict-JSON target, while weak action/subtask metrics remain the next modeling problem.
takeaways.current_scope: The selected-episode Qwen3-Omni diagnostic pilot is verified on the 96/16/16 split and now meets the 98% target for JSON validity; action/subtask quality remains weak, so current results are diagnostic baselines, not strong model-quality claims.
